L1.area: HTTP request handling and server-side document processing

L2.description: An XML External Entity (XXE) vulnerability exists in the XML parser used for processing incoming REST API requests. The parser does not properly restrict external entity references, allowing an attacker to send crafted XML payloads that can result in disclosure of confidential data, server-side request forgery (SSRF), port scanning, and denial of service.

L3.area: HTTP request handling and server-side document processing
L3.description: An XML External Entity (XXE) vulnerability exists in the XML parser used for processing incoming REST API requests. The parser does not properly restrict external entity references, allowing an attacker to send crafted XML payloads that can result in disclosure of confidential data, server-side request forgery (SSRF), port scanning, and denial of service.

ground_truth.reason: The REST server's XML parser creates an XMLReader via SAXParserFactory without disabling external entity processing, allowing attackers to submit crafted XML that triggers XXE attacks leading to data disclosure, SSRF, and denial of service.
